user.bio: Just trying to figure out the puzzle of financial markets

comment.body:
1) QE means that the Federal Reserve is buying longer-dates bonds to try to bring down long-term yields.
2) There have been more indirect buyers at auctions, meaning more foreign buyers than usual.
3) You wrote that China could sell bonds to try to send a message to the US.
4) May 7

So, working backwards, I think you are saying that ME buyers are the indirect buyers.  Administration officials are concerned about the rising yields, so they are already acting here and there.  But the rate decision is so far away that if all hell breaks loose there will be an emergency meeting to approve QE between now and then.
comment.raw_body:
1) QE means that the Federal Reserve is buying longer-dates bonds to try to bring down long-term yields.

4) May 7

So, working backwards, I think you are saying that ME buyers are the indirect buyers.  Administration officials are concerned about the rising yields, so they are already acting here and there.  But the rate decision is so far away that if all hell breaks loose there will be an emergency meeting to approve QE between now and then.
